resources.string: Phase 0 — build pipeline OK.\nReady for Phase 1 (sensing layer).

Lets ContentReg see which app is in the foreground, detect scrolling, and read on-screen text — used to enforce your feed-time budget and cover explicit content with a block screen. All processing happens on your device; screen contents are never sent anywhere.
Accessibility: OFF — tap below to enable
Overlay permission: OFF — needed to block
You\'ve reached your feed time budget for this hour.
Leave this app to keep using your phone.
Scrolling in these apps draws down the budget.
Grant these so the app can sense, block, and filter. You can change them anytime in system settings.
Detects the foreground app and scrolling (and reads on-screen text later).
Draws the block screen over a feed when your time budget runs out.
Blocks explicit domains on-device. No traffic leaves your phone.
Makes the app harder to delete: Android blocks uninstalling until you turn it off. Friction, not a lock — Safe Mode or a factory reset still work.
While active, blocks the app from being uninstalled until you turn it off here — so a moment of weakness can\'t undo your setup.

Turn this on to make ContentReg harder to remove. While active, Android won\'t let the app be uninstalled until you deactivate it — here in the app or in Settings. You can turn it off at any time. This does not lock your phone or read anything.
Turning this off removes ContentReg\'s uninstall protection, so the app can then be deleted. Are you sure?
On — the app can\'t be uninstalled until you turn this off.
Off — the app can be uninstalled normally.
Pin a home-screen shortcut with a photo you choose and your own label — it opens the app. (Stock Android can\'t change the built-in app icon to a photo; this is the supported alternative.)
Shortcut request sent — confirm it on your home screen.
Your launcher doesn\'t support pinning shortcuts.
Couldn\'t read that image. Try another.
Before you start: how ContentReg works

ContentReg helps you cut back on doomscrolling and avoid explicit content. To do that, it watches what happens on your screen. Please read this before turning anything on.

WHAT IT ACCESSES
• The app currently in the foreground, and when you scroll in it.
• The text and links visible on your screen, including your browser's address bar.
• The domain names (e.g. example.com) your device looks up while the URL filter is on.

WHY
• To measure time spent scrolling feed apps and enforce your hourly budget.
• To detect explicit on-screen text and cover it with a block screen.
• To block domains you choose to filter.

WHERE YOUR DATA GOES
• All of this is analysed on your device. The text on your screen and your browsing history are never uploaded, shared, or sent to us or any server.
• One honest exception: to filter domains, the app runs a local VPN that inspects DNS lookups. Allowed lookups are forwarded to a DNS resolver — your device's normal resolver, or a public one such as 8.8.8.8 — exactly as they would be without this app. That is ordinary internet networking, not us collecting your history, and the app keeps no remote log of it.

YOU'RE IN CONTROL
• Accessibility, Display-over-other-apps and VPN are granted by you and can be turned off at any time — in Android Settings or here in the app. Turning one off disables that feature.

By tapping "I understand & agree", you consent to the on-device monitoring described above.
